title: Build a Roblox tycoon in 60 seconds with AI
hook_text: watch me build a complete Roblox tycoon game without writing a single line of code
script_body: [screen recording already playing] I open RobloxMaxx. I type one sentence: 'pizza tycoon with 8 cooking stations, upgrade system, and gamepasses.' hit generate. [show code appearing] 15 scripts. ServerScriptService. ReplicatedStorage. StarterGui. everything connected. I copy all of it. paste into Roblox Studio. hit play. [show working game] it works. pizza tycoon. upgrades. gamepasses. 47 seconds. link in bio.
media_notes: Screen recording: split between RobloxMaxx interface and Roblox Studio. speed up the code generation slightly. show the working game at the end.

title: POV: You described your dream Roblox game and AI built it
hook_text: POV: you just described your dream Roblox game to an AI and it actually built the whole thing
script_body: [POV camera looking at monitor] I've always wanted to build a Roblox RPG. combat system. quests. inventory. the works. I type it out in plain English. [show typing] 'RPG with sword combat, 5 quests, inventory system, NPC shops, and experience levels.' I hit generate. [timer in corner] 90 seconds. 24 scripts. I paste them into Studio. [show game running] there's combat. there's quests. there's an inventory. this took me 90 seconds. last time I tried building this by hand it took 3 weeks and I gave up.
media_notes: POV style. face cam optional. emphasize the timer counting. show genuine reaction to working game.

title: 3 hours of coding vs 47 seconds with AI
hook_text: what 3 hours of Roblox scripting looks like vs 47 seconds with AI
script_body: [split screen] left side: me. 3 hours ago. writing tycoon code by hand. 200 lines. debugging RemoteEvents. fixing DataStore. coffee number 3. right side: me. today. I type one sentence. AI generates 400 lines of cleaner code. type annotations. proper cleanup. no memory leaks. 47 seconds. same game. better code. I'm not even mad. I'm relieved. [show both games running side by side] identical gameplay. one took 3 hours. one took 47 seconds.
media_notes: Split screen comparison. left side: frustrated coding montage (staged). right side: clean AI generation. end with both games running.

'good use of task.spawn instead of spawn.' 'DataStore implementation is solid.' 'who wrote this?' me: 'an AI. in 47 seconds.' title: Roblox tower defense in 73 seconds with AI
hook_text: watch me build a complete Roblox tower defense game from one sentence
script_body: [screen recording] tower defense. one of the hardest genres to code. wave spawning. tower placement. upgrade system. enemy pathfinding. usually takes weeks. I type: 'tower defense with 10 waves, 5 tower types, upgrade system, and enemy pathfinding along a path.' [show generation] 73 seconds. 22 scripts. I paste everything into Studio. [show working game] towers. enemies following the path. waves. upgrades. 73 seconds for a genre that usually takes weeks. I can't go back to manual coding after this.
media_notes: Emphasize that tower defense is complex. show the working pathfinding and tower placement.

title: The truth about free models on Roblox
hook_text: stop using free models on Roblox. here's why.
script_body: [serious tone] I downloaded the top 10 free model tycoon kits on Roblox. 4 of them had suspicious scripts. 7 used deprecated API. all 10 had zero type annotations. none of them handled DataStore properly. [show code examples] this is what you're putting in your game. vs this is what AI generates: [show clean code] typed. documented. no backdoors. no deprecated calls. proper error handling. the free model is not free. it costs you debugging time and player trust. generate clean code in 47 seconds instead.
media_notes: Show actual code comparison. red highlights on bad code. green on clean code. serious educational tone.

title: Month 1 recap: building Roblox games with AI
hook_text: 30 days of building Roblox games with AI. here's everything I learned.
script_body: [montage of clips from the month] 30 days. here's the truth about AI game development. 1: AI writes better boilerplate than I do. and I've been coding for years. [clip] 2: the time savings compound. day 1 I saved 3 hours. day 30 I saved 40+ hours total. [clip] 3: the games actually get players. 5 of my AI-generated games have active players right now. [clip] 4: the code quality is real. not a single DataStore bug in 30 days. [clip] 5: I should have started sooner. every day I hand-coded boilerplate was a day wasted. month 2 starts now.
media_notes: Montage format. clips from throughout the month. numbers on screen. end with forward-looking energy.
